name: pywharf
vendor: huntzhan (Hunt Zhan), pywharf org — community/individual project
cost: Free (MIT open source); no paid tiers or SaaS offering
language: Python (>=3.7,<4.0; FastAPI/uvicorn-style HTTP server, split into pywharf-core + pywharf-github packages)
first_released: 2020 (as pywharf 0.2.0, March 2020; project originated under the name "private-pypi")
last_updated: 2022-02-27 (GitHub repo archived/read-only; last PyPI release 0.2.3 on 2020-07-11)
perf_notes: No published benchmarks. Architecture offloads file bandwidth to the backend: with the GitHub backend the simple index links point directly at GitHub release-asset download URLs (clients download from GitHub, not pywharf), max file size 2GB (GitHub limit); filesystem backend default max file size 5GB. The server keeps a local index synced from a backend-stored "remote" index for fast reads.
scale_notes: Single-process server (default 0.0.0.0:8888); no HA/clustering and no documented multi-node story. Source of truth lives in the backend (GitHub release assets or local filesystem); a separate `update_index` service maintains the remote index and each server instance maintains a local synced copy, so read replicas pulling from a shared GitHub backend are conceivable but undocumented. `github.gen_gh_pages` can publish a fully static, public PEP 503 index to GitHub Pages (CDN-served, no pywharf process needed for reads). Project is archived/unmaintained since Feb 2022.
summary: An archived (2022), MIT-licensed self-hosted private PyPI server that bridges pip/poetry/twine to pluggable storage backends — notably GitHub releases (or local filesystem) — using per-repo read/write secrets over HTTP basic auth.
extras: GitHub-releases-as-storage backend: packages stored as GitHub release assets (PAT-secured), index links point straight at GitHub download URLs so file bandwidth is offloaded to GitHub
Static public index generation for GitHub Pages via `github.gen_gh_pages` — produces a fully static PEP 503 index served by GitHub's CDN (this is what enables anonymous/public read)
Pluggable backend SDK split across packages (pywharf-core + pywharf-github); designed so new storage backends can be added cheaply
Per-repo read_secret/write_secret pairs (admin secrets TOML) give separate read vs write access per package repo; GitHub backend can map permissions to GitHub teams
`POST /initialize` endpoint reconfigures the running server on the fly; `GET /index_mtime` reports last index sync time
`--extra_index_url` fallback (default https://pypi.org/simple/): redirects clients to upstream PyPI when a package is not found locally (redirect-based fallback, not a caching proxy)
Environment-variable configuration mode (PYWHARF_COMMAND / PYWHARF_COMMAND_<FLAG>) as an alternative to passing CLI arguments, useful when shell args aren't feasible (e.g. in containers)
Browser auth via `/login` page storing credentials in a session cookie (`/logout` clears it), in addition to CLI HTTP basic auth
Renamed from the earlier project 'private-pypi'; GitHub repo archived (read-only) on 2022-02-27 and effectively unmaintained
